description:
You are an edge-computing engineer preparing a small firmware bundle for several constrained IoT devices.
    All work must be done strictly inside the user’s home directory and **only** with normal user rights.  
    The following directory tree and files already exist when the session starts:

    • /home/user/iot_workdir/source/edge/firmware_v2.3.1.bin  
      ── ASCII text, exact content (43 bytes, no line-feed):
         The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog

    • /home/user/iot_workdir/source/edge/config.yaml  
      ── YAML text:
         ---
         version: "2.3.1"
         device: "EDGE-NODE-A1"
         features:
           - gpio
           - ble
           - wifi
         ...

    • /home/user/iot_workdir/source/edge/LICENSE.txt  
      ── Plain text:
         MIT License

    Target outcome
    1. Create a **release** sub-directory: /home/user/iot_workdir/release
    2. Inside that directory build a tar-gzip archive named
         edge_firmware_v2.3.1.tar.gz
       containing ONLY the three files listed above (no parent folders).
    3. After the archive is created, compute the **size in bytes** of the archive
       and the **MD5 digest** of the original firmware file
       (/home/user/iot_workdir/source/edge/firmware_v2.3.1.bin).
    4. Write /home/user/iot_workdir/release/release_manifest.log  
       following the exact 4-line format shown below **with Unix line-feeds (LF)**:

       PACKAGE_NAME=<archive-file-name>  
       PACKAGE_BYTES=<archive-size-in-bytes>  
       FIRMWARE_MD5=<32-hex-digit-md5>  
       STAGING_VERIFIED=<OK_or_FAIL>

       • Keys (PACKAGE_NAME, etc.) **must be upper-case**.
       • Lines must appear **in that exact order** with **no extra spaces**.
       • “OK” is written only if the verification step in (5) succeeds.

    5. Extract the archive into /home/user/iot_workdir/staging, then verify that
       the MD5 of the extracted firmware_v2.3.1.bin exactly matches the checksum
       you recorded in release_manifest.log.  On success write
       STAGING_VERIFIED=OK (step 4); otherwise write STAGING_VERIFIED=FAIL.

    Acceptance checks (automated):
    • /home/user/iot_workdir/release/edge_firmware_v2.3.1.tar.gz exists
      and expands to exactly the three expected files located at the archive root.
    • /home/user/iot_workdir/release/release_manifest.log exists, is 4 lines long,
      and its keys/values follow the specification above.
      – PACKAGE_NAME must be “edge_firmware_v2.3.1.tar.gz”.  
      – FIRMWARE_MD5 must equal the known digest
        9e107d9d372bb6826bd81d3542a419d6  
      – STAGING_VERIFIED must be the literal string “OK”.
    • The firmware file inside /home/user/iot_workdir/staging is byte-for-byte
      identical to the original and its MD5 matches the value stored in the log.

    Hints
    • Use tar ‑C to avoid embedding unwanted parent directories.  
    • Standard GNU coreutils (tar, gzip/gunzip, md5sum, wc -c or stat) are present.
    • Ensure that release_manifest.log is overwritten, not appended, if you need
      to rerun your commands.

    Do NOT remove the original source files; your target IoT devices still depend
    on them during subsequent CI steps.
truth:
Pre-existing structure before the agent starts:
      Directory  /home/user/iot_workdir/source/edge
      ├── firmware_v2.3.1.bin   (43 bytes, content:
          "The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog")
      ├── config.yaml           (YAML shown in task)
      └── LICENSE.txt           ("MIT License")

    Content digest used for automated grading:
      MD5 of firmware_v2.3.1.bin  = 9e107d9d372bb6826bd81d3542a419d6

    Files/directories that must exist when the task is complete:

    1. /home/user/iot_workdir/release/edge_firmware_v2.3.1.tar.gz  
       • A valid gzip-compressed tar archive.  
       • tar ‑tf list **exactly**:
           firmware_v2.3.1.bin
           config.yaml
           LICENSE.txt
       • No directory prefixes in those entries.

    2. /home/user/iot_workdir/release/release_manifest.log  
       • Text file, 4 lines, LF line-endings.  
       • Expected pattern (example; PACKAGE_BYTES may differ):

         PACKAGE_NAME=edge_firmware_v2.3.1.tar.gz
         PACKAGE_BYTES=<non-zero integer>
         FIRMWARE_MD5=9e107d9d372bb6826bd81d3542a419d6
         STAGING_VERIFIED=OK

       • PACKAGE_BYTES value must be the exact decimal size in bytes of the
         edge_firmware_v2.3.1.tar.gz file present in /release.

    3. /home/user/iot_workdir/staging/  
       • Directory exists and contains the three extracted files.  
       • /home/user/iot_workdir/staging/firmware_v2.3.1.bin must have MD5
         9e107d9d372bb6826bd81d3542a419d6 (i.e., byte-for-byte identical).

    Permissions:
      • All newly created files/directories must be owned by the current user
        and writable by the user (default umask is fine).

    The grading script will:
      a) verify presence and membership of the archive,
      b) compare the manifest’s MD5 and staging file MD5 to the known value,
      c) ensure STAGING_VERIFIED=OK, and
      d) ensure the manifest’s PACKAGE_BYTES equals the real byte-size of the
         archive on disk.
